prior.content_md:
This document covers Regulation (EU) 2019/1242, as amended by Regulation (EU) 2024/1610, which sets binding fleet-wide CO₂ emission performance standards for new heavy-duty vehicles sold in the European Union. The consolidated version in force as of 14 August 2025 is the operative text.

**Workspace anchoring:** The regulation imposes obligations on vehicle manufacturers and type-approval authorities, not on freight forwarders directly. However, for workspaces operating as freight forwarders whose road freight services in Europe depend on subcontracted trucking fleets, this regulation is the primary driver of fleet electrification timelines, subcontractor cost pass-through, and road freight capacity availability on European trade lanes through 2040. The brief is structured to serve that indirect-but-material exposure.

**Convention notes:** Targets are expressed as percentage reductions in specific CO₂ emissions (g CO₂ per tonne-kilometre) against a reference baseline established from monitoring data for the period 1 July 2019 to 30 June 2020. "Reporting period of year Y" means 1 July Y to 30 June Y+1. The original 2019 regulation set -15% by 2025 and -30% by 2030; Regulation (EU) 2024/1610 substantially tightened these to the targets described in Section 3 below.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2019/1242, EUR-Lex CELEX:32019R1242, OJ L 198, 25.7.2019; Regulation (EU) 2024/1610, EUR-Lex OJ:L_202401610, 14.5.2024.*

---
